items.title: Iran war unlikely to trigger terror attacks in Indonesia: ex-militants
US said to be withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany over Iran war spat
items.description: President Donald Trump has feuded with European allies over their reluctance to step up support for war on Iran.

items.title: Media Iran, 'Teheran ha consegnato una nuova proposta negoziale'
items.description: Agenzia Irna: 'Recapitata ieri ai mediatori pachistani'. Wsj: 'Dialogo sul nucleare in cambio di allentamento sanzioni'

items.title: Cuba calls US sanctions 'collective punishment' during May 1 marches
items.description: In an executive order on May 1, US President Donald Trump announced sanctions on people involved in wide-sweeping sectors of the Cuban economy, including those who operate in 'energy and 'defense.' Cuba's foreign minister called the new measures 'collective punishment' of the nation's people.

items.title: Hezbollah drones wound 4 troops; IDF urges civilians to leave south Lebanon town
items.description: Home Front Command tightens security guidelines in border communities amid continued attacks; Lebanese state media reports four, including a woman, killed in IDF strikes in south The post Hezbollah drones wound 4 troops; IDF urges civilians to leave south Lebanon town appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
items.description: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

items.title: President Macron attends Orion 26 excerices
items.description: In France, President Emmanuel Macron attended the final phase of the “Orion 26” military exercises on Thursday, a large-scale combat simulation involving thousands of troops from France and several European nations. The drills, which began in February, highlight a growing focus on military readiness across Europe amid …

items.title: Is Myanmar's military winning the civil war?
items.description: After five years of war, armed resistance groups still challenge Myanmar's military across much of the country. But the generals are now stemming losses and reclaiming some territory.

items.title: Zelenskyy says he's seeking details of Putin's May 9 ceasefire proposal
items.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is seeking details of a short-term ceasefire Russia proposed to U.S. President Donald Trump.

items.title: Kremlin Says Ukraine’s Consent Not Needed for Ceasefire as Zelensky Rejects ‘Theatre’
items.description: Zelensky called Moscow’s proposed Victory Day truce “manipulative” theater, while the Kremlin said the ceasefire will proceed regardless of Kyiv’s response.

items.title: Is our home safe? Is it rubble? For displaced Lebanese, anguished uncertainty.
items.description: Lebanese people displaced by war have been on an emotional roller coaster. A ceasefire has been extended, but fighting in the south hasn’t ended. Even as families yearning for home prepare to return, despite the uncertainty, they are warned not to take the risk.

items.title: Oil price tops $126 a barrel after Trump warns Iran blockade could last ‘months’
items.description: Markets spooked as US president appears willing to keep up naval blockade and Iran keeps strait of Hormuz all but shutBusiness live – latest updatesThe global oil price hit $126 a barrel on Thursday, its highest level since 2022, after Donald Trump said the US blockade of Iranian ports could last for months and peace t…

items.title: Lebanon strikes deepen crisis as hunger rises and conditions worsen in Gaza
items.description: At least nine people were reported to have been killed in southern Lebanon on Thursday, as ongoing hostilities continue to exact a heavy toll on civilians and drive a worsening humanitarian crisis marked by rising hunger and strained public services.

items.title: Ukraine Strikes Deep Inside Russia, Tuapse Hit Again
items.description: Ukraine has hit an oil facility and industrial site in Russia's Perm region, roughly 1,500 kilometers from the Ukrainian border, according to Russian officials, as US President Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin discussed a potential temporary cease-fire aimed at pausing the four-year war in Ukraine.

items.title: Putin, Trump Discuss Iran, Ukraine in Phone Call, Kremlin Aide Says
items.description: Putin offered a ceasefire in the fighting in Ukraine on May 9, when Russia celebrates the Soviet Union's World War II victory over Nazi Germany.
